forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over Bass Strait will continue to move slowly eastwards to be centred over the Tasman Sea on Monday. A trough is forecast to move across the west and south later Tuesday, pushing into the northeast of SA on Wednesday. Another high pressure system is expected to become established south of the Bight later in the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Dry apart from isolated showers and thunderstorms about the west. Maximum temperatures near to above average, but below average in the northeast. Generally east to northeasterly winds.
Dry apart from isolated showers and thunderstorms about the far southwest. Generally southeast to northeasterly winds.

Showers and thunderstorms, possibly severe, extending across the west, reaching central districts at night. Maximum temperatures above average. Northerly winds, freshening in the west ahead of a southwesterly change extending over western parts in the afternoon and evening.

Showers and morning thunderstorms moving across the agricultural area. Isolated showers to follow about coastal parts mostly clearing later. Maximum temperatures near to slightly above average, lowering to below average in the west. Southwest to southerly winds extending throughout.

text.p: Dry apart from possible isolated showers about the southern coasts. Areas of morning fog about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures near to slightly below average about the southern agricultural area, rising above average further inland. Southwest to southeasterly winds and locally fresh afternoon sea breezes.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: Less than 2mm, with highly variable totals of 5 to 30 mm across the west on Tuesday lowering to 2 to 10 mm about the southern agricultural area Wednesday morning.
